evidence.scientific_clarity: Sankey correctly shows 1000->820->600/120/100 flow with proportional band widths; no numeric labels on flows/nodes so counts must be inferred from caption alone.
evidence.typography: Arial used throughout (confirmed in SVG and PDF CMap); font size 13px body / 16px title is reasonable; no DejaVu Sans; but PDF uses kaleido raster-compositing for alpha bands (28 Image XObjects) so fonttype=42 concept doesn't apply — text IS selectable (CIDFontType2/Type0 embedded).
evidence.palette: Seven distinct hues; green (Cleaned) vs red (Filtered) encodes pass/reject which is directional-appropriate, but red-green pair fails deuteranopia; no legend — relies solely on node text labels.
evidence.layout_density: 820x480 canvas is well-used; three pipeline stages spread across width; node labels readable without overlap; Val and Test nodes are narrow and close together at right edge but not overlapping.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and cites specific counts; no legend for node colors; no numeric annotations on flow bands (reviewer will likely ask for counts on links); 'implicitly absorbed by rounding' note in caption is awkward.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains embedded ArialMT (CIDFontType2/Type0) with selectable text confirmed via CMap decoding; Sankey link bands use alpha-composited Image XObjects (kaleido limitation) but text and node rectangles are true vectors.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (70KB), figure.png (129KB @2x), figure.svg (8.2KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific counts (1000 raw, 120 rejected at ingestion, 60 during cleaning, 820 cleaned, 600 train / 120 val / 100 test) and explains flow-width encoding; covers the key numerical finding.

overall_strengths: All required output files present; PDF has selectable embedded Arial text (not rasterized labels); caption provides concrete numerical claims matching the figure.
Clean Plotly Sankey layout with consistent source-color coding for flows, proportional band widths, and a readable title.
overall_weaknesses: No numeric annotations on flow bands — readers cannot read off counts without consulting the caption, and a reviewer will likely request them.
Red-green color pair (Cleaned=green, Filtered=red) fails deuteranopia colorblind safety; alpha-composited link bands are rasterized Image XObjects in the PDF rather than true vector paths.
